name: Project Soli
short_description: A miniature 60 GHz radar chip from Google ATAP that senses fine touchless hand micro-gestures and human presence.
long_description: Soli is a millimeter-wave frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radar sensor and antenna array shrunk onto a single chip a few millimeters across. Announced at Google I/O 2015 and led by Ivan Poupyrev, it detects sub-millimeter hand motions at very high frame rates without cameras, light, or contact, and works through materials. It moved from a developer kit into consumer products: 'Motion Sense' touchless gestures and presence detection in the 2019 Pixel 4, and contactless 'Sleep Sensing' (breathing and movement tracking) in the 2nd-gen Nest Hub in 2021. As physical AI, Soli is significant as ambient, infrastructure-grade sensing: the device perceives gesture and presence while staying invisible, raising it as a privacy-friendlier alternative to cameras for in-the-environment interaction.

images.caption: Front schematic of the Google Pixel 4, the first consumer phone to ship the Soli radar (Motion Sense) behind the top bezel.
images.alt_text: A line schematic of the front of a Google Pixel 4 smartphone, with a wide top bezel that houses the Soli radar sensor used for Motion Sense.

sources.title: Soli: Ubiquitous Gesture Sensing with Millimeter Wave Radar
sources.publisher: ACM Transactions on Graphics (SIGGRAPH), Vol. 35, No. 4
sources.access_notes: Authors: Jaime Lien, Nicholas Gillian, M. Emre Karagozler, Patrick Amihood, Carsten Schwesig, Erik Olson, Hakim Raja, Ivan Poupyrev (Google ATAP). Primary technical reference for the Soli FMCW radar approach.

sources.title: Soli: Radar-Based Perception and Interaction in Pixel 4
sources.access_notes: Describes 60 GHz FMCW radar, Motion Sense and presence detection in Pixel 4.

sources.title: Google's Soli radar returns to track sleep on the new Nest Hub

sources.title: Google ATAP hopes its Project Soli and Project Jacquard will change how we interact with wearables
